the fraction $\frac{100!}{10^{50}}$ is written as an irreducible fraction, meaning that the numerator and denominator have no common factors other than 1. to find the denominator of this irreducible fraction, we need to determine the prime factorization of $100!$ and divide it by $10^{50}$. the prime factorization of 100! includes all the prime numbers up to 100, each raised to a certain power. the denominator of the irreducible fraction will be the product of the prime factors of $100!$ that are not canceled out by the denominator $10^{50}$. 

the prime factorization of $10^{50}$ is $2^{50} \times 5^{50}$. this means that in the denominator, we need to remove all factors of 2 and 5 from the prime factorization of $100!$. the remaining prime factors in the denominator will be the ones that are not canceled out by the denominator. 

to find the denominator of the irreducible fraction, we need to calculate the exponents of the prime factors of $100!$ and subtract the exponents of 2 and 5 from the denominator. the exponents of 2 and 5 in the prime factorization of $100!$ can be calculated using the formula for exponents of primes in factorials. the exponent of a prime $p$ in $n!$ is given by $\sum_{k=1}^{\infty} \left\lfloor \frac{n}{p^k} \right\rfloor$.
